title: ebenidium lagopus
intro: Ebenidium lagopus, also known as the larch or tamarack, is a type of evergreen coniferous tree native to northern parts of the world. The bark of the Ebenidium lagopus tree contains a compound called betulin, which has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ebenidium lagopus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its use in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Ebenidium lagopus tree is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to soothe and protect the skin.
